attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches from 150-200m after being delivered through final corners. Relies on late positioning advantage rather than raw sustained speed, timing the sprint from the straightaway after technical corners.
team_tactics: DSM/Sunweb deploys specialized corner-entry leadouts, with key domestiques (Benoot noted) holding position through final turns at 200m before release. Team struggles when illness or attrition disrupts primary sprint plan, showing limited backup options.
terrain_preference: Technical sprint finishes with right-hand corners inside 300m that allow late positioning gains. Exposed on pure power sprints after reduced bunch scenarios or when leadout train is disrupted mid-finale.
vulnerability: Cannot contest effectively from reduced bunches after hard stages (213km Paris-Nice). Hesitates when sprint favorites are eliminated mid-effort. Illness and form inconsistency disrupt preparation. Loses to superior raw finishing speed even from optimal position.
key_patterns: Corner-entry leadout specialization: team holds through final turn, releases at 150-200m (2 observations)
Wins require perfect positioning; unable to salvage results from compromised leadouts (3+ observations)
Long gaps between wins suggest inconsistent form or limited opportunism (February 2020 to March 2021)
Team lacks depth: illness or Plan A failure leaves no alternative sprint setup (2 observations)
Third-wheel position inside 1km is optimal setup, but still loses to faster finishers
form_trajectory: negative: No wins recorded after March 2021 victory. Showed inability to contest sprints by 2022 Paris-Nice/Giro, suggesting declining competitiveness or persistent form/health issues.
